protocol.name: Morpho Midnight
protocol.type: EVM non-custodial fixed-rate fixed-maturity lending protocol
protocol.repo_focus: Solidity protocol with core contract, interfaces, libraries, periphery, and ratifiers
main_design: offer-based trading instead of pooled liquidity
makers do not lock capital when publishing offers
optional maker callback can source funds during take
optional market gates restrict entry or liquidation

units.one_debt_unit: obligation to repay one loan token before maturity
units.buying_units: increases credit or reduces existing debt first
units.selling_units: increases debt or reduces existing credit first

maturity.post_maturity_trading_purpose: facilitate unwinding when liquidations are unprofitable

solvency: contract token balances must cover withdrawable assets, collateral claims, credit redemptions, and accrued fees
total claimable credit must not exceed repaid loan assets plus valid recoverable debt after loss accounting
bad debt must reduce lender credit exactly once and proportionally
credit_debt: every credit increase must correspond to valid debt creation, debt reduction, or settled repayment state
every debt increase must pass collateral and gate checks
buying units must reduce existing debt before increasing credit
selling units must reduce existing credit before increasing debt
debt must not increase after maturity
offers: offer fill amount cannot exceed remaining offer capacity
consumption group budget cannot be exceeded across offers
cancelled, expired, invalid, or already-consumed offers cannot be filled
ratifier approval must bind correct maker, market, offer, amount, price, deadline, nonce, salt, and consumption group
partial fills must update consumed state before any exploitable reentry point
collateral: collateral cannot be withdrawn if it would make debt unhealthy
collateral cannot be seized unless liquidation rules allow it
collateral bitmap/list state must match actual deposited collateral
multi-collateral valuation must not skip or double count collateral
liquidation: healthy positions cannot be health-liquidated before maturity
overdue debt after maturity must be liquidatable
repay amount, seize amount, LIF, close factor, dust, and bad-debt realization must be mutually consistent
liquidation must not leave accounting desynced between borrower debt, lender credit, protocol balances, and seized collateral
maturity: pre-maturity and post-maturity paths must not be composable to bypass debt repayment
maturity boundary must not allow debt increase by rounding, timestamp equality, or callback sequencing
post-maturity unwinding must not mint free credit or erase debt incorrectly
auth_and_gates: authorization must only allow intended account delegation
gate checks must apply to all entry paths
exit paths must remain available even when enter gate reverts
liquidator gate must not be bypassed through alternate liquidation or callback paths
external_calls: callbacks must not corrupt partial state through reentrancy
ERC20 transfer deltas must match accounting deltas
fee-on-transfer, rebasing, false-return, ERC777-like hooks, and non-standard decimals should be tested if not explicitly excluded
multicall must not bypass per-action invariants

high_value_attack_flows.name: deposit_take_withdraw_same_tx
sequence: callback or multicall tries to withdraw collateral

high_value_attack_flows.name: partial_fill_over_consumption
sequence: fill again through same or related offer
assert consumed amount and group budget cannot exceed cap

high_value_attack_flows.name: callback_reentrancy_take
sequence: assert no partial state can be reused

high_value_attack_flows.name: maturity_boundary_trade
sequence: assert debt cannot increase after maturity

high_value_attack_flows.name: healthy_to_liquidatable_rounding
sequence: assert healthy position cannot be liquidated

high_value_attack_flows.name: bad_debt_double_realization
sequence: assert lender credit loss is not double-counted or skipped

high_value_attack_flows.name: gate_bypass_entry
sequence: attempt direct entry and indirect entry via callback/multicall/authorization
assert every credit/debt increase hits enter gate

high_value_attack_flows.name: liquidator_gate_bypass
sequence: attempt liquidation through alternate actor/callback/authorized address
assert only approved liquidator can liquidate

high_value_attack_flows.name: coarse_authorization_abuse
sequence: assert behavior matches documented full-control model and cannot be confused by integrations

high_value_attack_flows.name: fee_crystallization_edge
sequence: assert old credit fee terms are crystallized and not retroactively changed

highest_priority_questions: Can credit/debt/collateral accounting desync from actual ERC20 balances?
Can callback or multicall observe and reuse partial state?
Can an offer or consumption group be overfilled across multiple markets?
Can maturity boundary allow debt increase or invalid settlement?
Can liquidation seize too much, too little, or realize bad debt incorrectly?
Can gates or ratifiers be bypassed through alternate entry paths?
Can rounding near LLTV/LIF/dust/tick/fee boundaries create extractable value?
Can non-standard token behavior break accounting assumptions?

valid_finding_requires: Foundry PoC or stateful fuzz reproduction
likely_invalid_if: only causes failed transaction for attacker
only proves integration misuse without protocol-level bug
matches documented coarse authorization behavior without additional confusion bug
